axioms.name: Attention as Ur-Currency
axioms.statement: Attention is the fundamental currency of all narrative; all media solve the problem of sustaining engagement through time.

axioms.name: Pure Attention through Involuntary Response
axioms.statement: The purest attention mechanisms are those producing involuntary physiological responses (laughter in comedy, fear in horror).

axioms.name: Causal Binding as Attention Glue
axioms.statement: Regardless of medium, causal binding (But/Therefore) is the underlying adhesive that sustains momentum and interest.

axioms.name: Density as Compensation
axioms.statement: Weak narrative causation requires high attention-sustaining density (visual/audio) to compensate; strong causation can operate with lower density.

levels.name: ATTENTION_ARC
levels.description: The complete duration of engagement from open to close

levels.name: MOMENTUM_BLOCK
levels.description: A meso-unit (scene, page, level) that sustains focus through a specific mechanism

levels.name: ATTENTION_UNIT
levels.description: The micro-unit of attention (word, panel, frame, input)

core_algorithms.name: Universal Attention Diagnostic
core_algorithms.purpose: Evaluate any narrative's ability to sustain engagement across its duration
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Identify micro-unit; 2. Test causal binding (reorderability); 3. Identify prediction mode (Jazz vs Anticipation); 4. Assess density compensation; RETURN engagement_risk;

core_algorithms.name: Involuntary Response Test
core_algorithms.purpose: Verify the purity of a genre work by somatic response overcoming resistance
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF genre == COMEDY: IF audience_resists AND audience_laughs: RETURN PURE; IF genre == HORROR: IF brave_audience AND audience_feels_fear: RETURN PURE; ELSE: RETURN CONTRIVED;

core_algorithms.name: Prediction Mode Assessment
core_algorithms.purpose: Classify narrative operation as Anticipation-Satisfaction or Prediction-Failure-Recalibration (Jazz)
core_algorithms.pseudocode: FOR each structural_level: IF subversion_frequency == HIGH: mode = JAZZ; ELIF subversion_frequency == LOW: mode = ANTICIPATION; RETURN mode_map;

core_algorithms.name: Parallel Stream Audit
core_algorithms.purpose: Measure the information density available to sustain attention at any moment
core_algorithms.pseudocode: COUNT active_channels (Visual, Audio, Semantic, Kinetic); EVALUATE channel_autonomy; IF any_channel_failure: CHECK if other_channels_carry_attention; RETURN resilience_score;

diagnostic_questions.question: Does every micro-moment provide a justification for continued attention?
Does the work target an involuntary physiological response (Tier 1)?
Is causal binding (But/Therefore) strong enough to create momentum?
Is information density high enough to compensate for any structural weaknesses?
Which prediction mode (Anticipation vs Jazz) is dominant at the beat level?
Would the audience disengage if one sensory channel (e.g., audio) were removed?
